content.text: Now let me fetch the sample metadata for GSE192391:

input.code:
import urllib.request, re

def geo_supp_files(acc):
    """List supplementary files for a GEO accession."""
    url = (f"https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/query/acc.cgi"
           f"?acc={acc}&targ=self&form=text&view=quick")
    txt = urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=60).read().decode("utf-8", "replace")
    urls = re.findall(r"^![A-Za-z]+_supplementary_file_\d+\s*=\s*(\S+)", txt, re.M)
    return [u.replace("ftp://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov", "https://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov") for u in urls]

# Fetch metadata for GSE192391
acc = "GSE192391"
url = f"https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/query/acc.cgi?acc={acc}&targ=self&form=text&view=quick"
txt = urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=60).read().decode("utf-8", "replace")

# Extract series title
title_match = re.search(r"^!Series_title\s*=\s*(.+)$", txt, re.M)
series_title = title_match.group(1) if title_match else "N/A"

# Extract sample IDs and titles
samples = re.findall(r"^!Series_sample_id\s*=\s*(\w+)$", txt, re.M)
print(f"Series: {acc}")
print(f"Title: {series_title}")
print(f"Number of samples: {len(samples)}")
print("\nSample accessions found:")
for s in samples:
    print(f"  {s}")

input.code:
import GEOparse
import pandas as pd

# Fetch the GSE192391 series metadata
gse = GEOparse.get_GEO("GSE192391", destdir="./geo_meta", silent=True)

# Get all sample GSM accessions
gsm_keys = sorted(gse.gsms.keys())
print(f"GSE192391: {gse.metadata.get('title', ['N/A'])[0]}")
print(f"Number of samples: {len(gsm_keys)}\n")

# Build a table of sample metadata
sample_data = []
for gsm_id in gsm_keys:
    gsm = gse.gsms[gsm_id]
    meta = gsm.metadata
    
    sample_data.append({
        'Sample ID': gsm_id,
        'Title': meta.get('title', [''])[0],
        'Organism': meta.get('organism_ch1', [''])[0],
        'Cell Type': meta.get('characteristics_ch1', [''])[0],
        'Characteristics': '; '.join(meta.get('characteristics_ch1', []))
    })

df_samples = pd.DataFrame(sample_data)
print(df_samples.to_string(index=False))

# Save to CSV for reference
df_samples.to_csv('./GSE192391_samples.csv', index=False)
print(f"\nSample table saved to GSE192391_samples.csv")

Perfect! Here's the **sample table for GSE192391**:

## GSE192391: Single cell transcriptomics of PBMCs during severe COVID-19

**30 samples** from COVID-19 patients and healthy controls. The dataset includes:

| Sample ID | Title | Patient | Tissue | Cell Type | Disease State | Status | Age | Sex | Ethnicity | Time Point |
|-----------|-------|---------|--------|-----------|---------------|--------|-----|-----|-----------|------------|
| GSM5746259 | MGI0369_1_SLAB-145-0 | 145 | Blood | PBMC | COVID-19 | Alive | 70 | M | African American | Day 0 |
| GSM5746260 | MGI0369_1_SLAB-145-7 | 145 | Blood | PBMC | COVID-19 | Alive | 70 | M | African American | Day 7 |
| GSM5746261 | MGI0369_1_SLAB-154-0 | 154 | Blood | PBMC | COVID-19 | Alive | 71 | M | African American | Day 0 |
| GSM5746262 | MGI0369_1_SLAB-154-7 | 154 | Blood | PBMC | COVID-19 | Alive | 71 | M | African American | Day 7 |
| GSM5746263 | MGI0369_1_SLAB-163-0 | 163 | Blood | PBMC | COVID-19 | Deceased | 65 | M | Caucasian | Day 0 |
| GSM5746264 | MGI0369_1_SLAB-163-7 | 163 | Blood | PBMC | COVID-19 | Deceased | 65 | M | Caucasian | Day 7 |
| GSM5746265 | MGI0369_1_SLAB-251-0 | 251 | Blood | PBMC | COVID-19 | Deceased | 87 | M | African American | Day 0 |
| GSM5746266 | MGI0369_1_SLAB-251-7 | 251 | Blood | PBMC | COVID-19 | Deceased | 87 | M | African American | Day 7 |
| GSM5746267 | MGI0369_1_SLAB-85-7 | 85 | Blood | PBMC | COVID-19 | Deceased | 77 | M | Caucasian | Day 7 |
| GSM5746268–273 | (Control samples C1–C8) | Control | Blood | PBMC | Control | Various | 59–82 | M/F | Mixed | NA |
| GSM5746274+ | (Additional COVID-19 samples) | 272+ | Blood | PBMC | COVID-19 | Deceased | 83+ | F | Various | Day 0/7 |

**Key features:**
- **Subjects**: 5 COVID-19 patients (with paired day 0 & day 7 timepoints) + deceased status for some, plus 6 healthy controls
- **Organism**: Homo sapiens
- **Sample type**: Peripheral Blood Mononuclear Cells (PBMCs)
- **Metadata**: Patient ID, age, sex, ethnicity, clinical outcome (alive/deceased), and sampling timepoint

A full CSV table has been saved and is available above. Would you like me to download the expression data or perform any analysis on this dataset?

I'll fetch the count matrices for the first two samples (GSM5746259 and GSM5746260) and register them as a dataset.

input.code:
import GEOparse
import os
import shutil

# Create a directory for the data
data_dir = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), 'GSE192391_first_two_samples')
os.makedirs(data_dir, exist_ok=True)

# Fetch the first two samples
sample_ids = ['GSM5746259', 'GSM5746260']
print(f"Fetching count matrices for: {sample_ids}\n")

for gsm_id in sample_ids:
    print(f"Fetching {gsm_id}...")
    gsm = GEOparse.get_GEO(geo=gsm_id, destdir="./geo_fetch", silent=True)
    
    # Print available files
    print(f"  Title: {gsm.metadata.get('title', [''])[0]}")
    print(f"  Available supplementary files:")
    
    if hasattr(gsm, 'table') and gsm.table is not None:
        print(f"    - Expression table shape: {gsm.table.shape}")
        # Save the table
        output_file = os.path.join(data_dir, f'{gsm_id}_counts.csv')
        gsm.table.to_csv(output_file)
        print(f"    - Saved to {gsm_id}_counts.csv")
    
    # Check for supplementary files
    if 'supplementary_file' in gsm.metadata:
        for i, url in enumerate(gsm.metadata.get('supplementary_file', [])):
            print(f"    - Supplementary file {i}: {url}")
    
    print()

# List what we have
print(f"\nFiles in {data_dir}:")
for f in os.listdir(data_dir):
    filepath = os.path.join(data_dir, f)
    size = os.path.getsize(filepath)
    print(f"  {f} ({size:,} bytes)")

content.text: The GEOparse tables are empty. Let me check what supplementary files are actually available from GEO:

input.code:
import urllib.request
import re
import os
import subprocess

# Get supplementary files for the first sample
def get_supplementary_urls(gsm_id):
    """Fetch supplementary file URLs for a GSM."""
    url = (f"https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/query/acc.cgi"
           f"?acc={gsm_id}&targ=self&form=text&view=quick")
    txt = urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=60).read().decode("utf-8", "replace")
    
    # Extract supplementary file URLs
    urls = re.findall(r"^!Sample_supplementary_file_\d+\s*=\s*(\S+)", txt, re.M)
    return [u.replace("ftp://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov", "https://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov") for u in urls]

# Check first two samples
sample_ids = ['GSM5746259', 'GSM5746260']

for gsm_id in sample_ids:
    print(f"\n{gsm_id} - Supplementary files:")
    files = get_supplementary_urls(gsm_id)
    if files:
        for f in files:
            print(f"  {f}")
    else:
        print("  (None found at sample level)")

content.text: Perfect! Now let me download the 10x matrix files (the standard scRNA-seq format with matrix, barcodes, and features):

input.code:
import os
import subprocess

# Create data directory
data_dir = './GSE192391_first_two_samples'
os.makedirs(data_dir, exist_ok=True)

# Define URLs for both samples
sample_files = {
    'GSM5746259': [
        'https://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/samples/GSM5746nnn/GSM5746259/suppl/GSM5746259_MGI0369_1_SLAB-145-0.barcodes.tsv.gz',
        'https://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/samples/GSM5746nnn/GSM5746259/suppl/GSM5746259_MGI0369_1_SLAB-145-0.features.tsv.gz',
        'https://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/samples/GSM5746nnn/GSM5746259/suppl/GSM5746259_MGI0369_1_SLAB-145-0.matrix.mtx.gz',
    ],
    'GSM5746260': [
        'https://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/samples/GSM5746nnn/GSM5746260/suppl/GSM5746260_MGI0369_1_SLAB-145-7.barcodes.tsv.gz',
        'https://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/samples/GSM5746nnn/GSM5746260/suppl/GSM5746260_MGI0369_1_SLAB-145-7.features.tsv.gz',
        'https://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/samples/GSM5746nnn/GSM5746260/suppl/GSM5746260_MGI0369_1_SLAB-145-7.matrix.mtx.gz',
    ]
}

# Download each file with resumable transfer and progress
print("Downloading count matrices...\n")
for gsm_id, urls in sample_files.items():
    print(f"{gsm_id}:")
    for url in urls:
        filename = os.path.basename(url)
        filepath = os.path.join(data_dir, filename)
        
        # Use curl with resumable transfer (-C -)
        cmd = f"curl -L -C - -o '{filepath}' '{url}'"
        result = subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, capture_output=True, text=True)
        
        if result.returncode == 0:
            size_kb = os.path.getsize(filepath) / 1024
            print(f"  ✓ {filename} ({size_kb:.1f} KB)")
        else:
            print(f"  ✗ {filename} - FAILED")
            print(f"    Error: {result.stderr}")

print(f"\nAll files saved to: {data_dir}")
print(f"\nContents:")
for f in sorted(os.listdir(data_dir)):
    filepath = os.path.join(data_dir, f)
    size_kb = os.path.getsize(filepath) / 1024
    print(f"  {f} ({size_kb:.1f} KB)")

content.text: Excellent! The files are downloaded. Now let me verify they load properly and then register the dataset:

input.code:
import scanpy as sc
import pandas as pd
import anndata as ad
import os

data_dir = './GSE192391_first_two_samples'

# Function to load 10x triplet (loose, GSM-prefixed layout)
def load_geo_10x(prefix):
    """Load a GEO-style 10x triplet: matrix.mtx.gz, barcodes.tsv.gz, features.tsv.gz"""
    a = sc.read_mtx(f"{data_dir}/{prefix}.matrix.mtx.gz").T  # mtx is genes×cells → transpose
    a.obs_names = pd.read_csv(f"{data_dir}/{prefix}.barcodes.tsv.gz", header=None)[0].values
    a.var_names = pd.read_csv(f"{data_dir}/{prefix}.features.tsv.gz", header=None, sep='\t')[1].values  # col 1 = gene symbols
    a.var_names_make_unique()
    return a

# Load both samples
print("Loading count matrices...\n")
prefixes = [
    'GSM5746259_MGI0369_1_SLAB-145-0',
    'GSM5746260_MGI0369_1_SLAB-145-7'
]

sample_names = ['Patient_145_Day0', 'Patient_145_Day7']
adatas = []

for prefix, name in zip(prefixes, sample_names):
    print(f"Loading {name}...")
    adata = load_geo_10x(prefix)
    adata.obs['sample'] = name
    print(f"  Shape: {adata.n_obs} cells × {adata.n_vars} genes")
    adatas.append(adata)
    print()

# Concatenate into one object
print("Concatenating samples...")
adata_combined = ad.concat(adatas, label='sample', keys=sample_names, index_unique='-')
print(f"Combined shape: {adata_combined.n_obs} cells × {adata_combined.n_vars} genes")
print(f"Samples: {adata_combined.obs['sample'].unique().tolist()}")

# Save as h5ad for registration
output_h5ad = os.path.join(data_dir, 'GSE192391_first_two_samples_combined.h5ad')
adata_combined.write_h5ad(output_h5ad)
print(f"\nSaved combined object to: {os.path.basename(output_h5ad)}")

content.text:
Perfect! ✅ **Dataset registered successfully.**

## Summary

I've fetched the count matrices for the first two samples from **GSE192391** and registered them as a new dataset:

**Dataset details:**
- **Dataset ID**: dat_dfcfafe2
- **Title**: GSE192391 COVID-19 PBMC scRNA-seq - Patient 145 (Day 0 & 7)
- **Dimensions**: 13,846 cells × 36,601 genes
- **Samples**: 
  - GSM5746259 (Patient 145, Day 0) — 7,532 cells
  - GSM5746260 (Patient 145, Day 7) — 6,314 cells
- **Format**: 10x MTX triplets (matrix, barcodes, features) + combined HDF5 (.h5ad)
- **Source**: GEO GSE192391 (single COVID-19 patient with paired timepoints; patient survived)
